name: doccano
tagline: Open-source text annotation tool for building labeled NLP datasets
metaDescription: doccano is an open-source text annotation tool for classification, sequence labeling, and seq2seq tasks. Create projects, upload data, and label collaboratively.
overview: doccano is an open-source tool for annotating text by hand. It supports three kinds of tasks: text classification, sequence labeling, and sequence-to-sequence. With it you can build labeled datasets for jobs like sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, and text summarization.

It is aimed at teams who need training data for natural language processing. You create a project, upload your raw text, define labels, and start annotating in the browser. Multiple people can work on the same project together, and a REST API lets you read and write data programmatically.

Within data labeling tools, doccano focuses on text rather than images or audio. It runs as a self-hosted web app, so your data stays on your own machine or server, and it ships with SQLite by default or PostgreSQL for larger setups.
features: Annotation for text classification, sequence labeling, and sequence-to-sequence tasks
Collaborative annotation so several people can label one project together
RESTful API for reading and writing annotation data
Multi-language support, plus emoji and dark theme
Mobile support for annotating on smaller screens
Self-hosted via pip, Docker, or Docker Compose, with SQLite or PostgreSQL

gettingStarted.intro: doccano can run via pip (Python 3.8+), Docker, or Docker Compose. The pip path is the quickest way to get a local server running.

steps.heading: Install with pip
steps.body: Install doccano into a Python 3.8+ environment.

steps.heading: Initialize and create a user
steps.body: Set up the database and an admin account.
steps.code:
doccano init
doccano createuser --username admin --password pass

steps.heading: Start the web server
steps.body: Run the server, then visit http://127.0.0.1:8000/ in your browser.

steps.heading: Start the task queue
steps.body: In another terminal, start the task queue that handles file upload and download.

useCases: Labeling text for sentiment analysis or other classification models
Tagging entities for named entity recognition training data
Creating summary or translation pairs for sequence-to-sequence tasks
Running a shared annotation project where a team labels data together
